title: Thomas Cromwell by Diarmaid MacCulloch Book Review - I Do book requests BTW

enrichment.summary: Kyle reviews the audiobook 'Thomas Cromwell' by Diarmaid MacCulloch (26+ hours, listened at 2x speed), admitting he barely followed the dense detail but sharing the overall vibe. He covers Cromwell's role under Henry VIII, Henry's brutal reign and six wives, then digresses widely into the fall of Rome, wealth inequality, the Protestant Reformation, and comparisons between English and Japanese civilization.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: almost impossible to pay attention to — too much personal detail of too many people; barely remembers anything
a complete psychopath and brutal totalitarian; hopes he burns in hell 10,000 years
probably comfort-eating from emotional torment and a guilty conscience, not inherent gluttony
a truly nice guy who was falsely accused; the betrayal ate away at him and caused his physical decline
caused substantially by wealth inequality — the army that sacked it was largely liberated Roman slaves, i.e. it literally fell apart from the inside
England was far more advanced — outlawing torture while Japan was still codifying what a knight (samurai) was
ideologically more Catholic than Protestant; broke with Rome only because he wanted to divorce freely, not out of genuine religious conviction
fully open to reading and reviewing any big classic history book requested by viewers

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire
